reviewed.by: New reel from answers/did-jesus-really-exist. apologia-citations (registered) = 0 fabrications: Tacitus Annals 15.44 (senator; Christus executed under Pilate), Josephus Testimonium + brother-James passage (Ant. 20.9.1), Pliny/Suetonius/Lucian/Talmud, Ehrman (agnostic) 'Did Jesus Exist?' 2012 all verified. argument+orthodoxy via general-purpose running each agent's .md = argument WEAK->fixed (softened the grouped-sources line 'all take him as real' -> 'corroborate the picture', since Pliny/Suetonius/Lucian corroborate rather than independently attest; 'A executed'->'An executed'); orthodoxy CLEAN (historicity only; 'debate who he was, not whether' brackets Christology).

voiceover: Did Jesus really exist — or is he just a myth the church invented? Almost no historian agrees with the myth. Across the spectrum, believer and atheist alike, scholars treat his existence as settled. Tacitus, a Roman senator, records that Christus was executed under Pontius Pilate. The Jewish historian Josephus mentions Jesus — and the execution of his brother James. Pliny, Suetonius, Lucian, the Talmud — a chain of Roman and Jewish sources corroborate the picture. Even the agnostic scholar Bart Ehrman wrote a whole book refuting the idea that Jesus never existed. And here's the tell: no one invents a crucified messiah. An executed criminal was a scandal, not a marketing plan. You can debate who Jesus was. That he lived is not seriously in doubt.
